tactical_shape: Disciplined, balanced team that defended compactly and stayed well organised
Tight midfield shape that suffocated Turkey and protected a 2-0 lead
Defence-first approach after going ahead, with substitutes sitting deep to help preserve the result
standout_players: Patrick Beach 8/10 — dream World Cup debut; tipped Bardakci's shot onto the post and made two crucial free-kick saves
Alessandro Circati 7/10 — calm, strong in individual challenges, powerful in the air
Harry Souttar 8/10 — captained the side and headed or booted away anything that entered the box
Cameron Burgess 7/10 — muscled away attackers; described as a brick wall on the left
Jacob Italiano 8/10 — kept Yilmaz quiet, blocked crosses and shots, earned a standing ovation
Jordan Bos 7/10 — defensively disciplined and kept Arda Guler quiet for 45 minutes
Aiden O’Neill 6/10 — tidy circuit-breaker who kept the team ticking
Paul Okon-Engstler 7/10 — grew in confidence and played the long ball for Irankunda's opener
Connor Metcalfe 8/10 — huge energy and pressing, scored Australia's second
Nestory Irankunda 8/10 — scored a stunning solo goal with lightning pace
home_read.momentum: A convincing 2-0 win and a highly disciplined defensive performance leave Australia with confidence and momentum after spoiling the pre-match hype.

tactical_shape: Controlled tempo through Hakan Calhanoglu as main ball-carrier
Attacked more through transitions and right-back forward runs from Zeki Celik
Looked more dangerous after the break when Kenan Yildiz came on and immediately lifted the attack down the left
standout_players: Ugurcan Cakir 7/10 — commanding in the box and barely put a foot wrong aside from the goals
Abdulkerim Bardakci 7/10 — calm under pressure and Turkey's best defender
Hakan Calhanoglu 7/10 — main ball-carrier who controlled the tempo and connected lines
Arda Guler 8/10 — Turkey's most creative attacking player, though short of clear openings
Kenan Yildiz 7/10 — immediately elevated Turkey's attack down the left
Zeki Celik 6/10 — made threatening forward runs from right-back but was beaten for the opener
away_read.momentum: Despite pre-match talk of being more talented, Turkey were frustrated and largely blunted; the loss exposes a lack of cutting edge even if the more dynamic substitutes hinted at upside.
